questions.question: Which of the following is a correct use of the universal quantifier in mathematics?
options: All cats are animals.
The sum of all positive integers is 1.
Every student has a name.
The product of all numbers is 0.
questions.explanation: The universal quantifier is used to make a statement that applies to every member of a set. In this case, the correct answer is 'Every student has a name.'

questions.question: What is the function of the existential quantifier?
options: To make a statement that applies to every member of a set.
To make a statement that applies to at least one member of a set.
To make a statement that is always true.
To make a statement that is always false.
questions.explanation: The existential quantifier is used to make a statement that applies to at least one member of a set. In other words, it asserts the existence of at least one element that satisfies the given condition.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is an example of using 'for all'?
options: There is a cat in this room.
All numbers greater than 2 are odd.
This book is on the table.
There are students in this class.
questions.explanation: The statement 'All numbers greater than 2 are odd.' uses the universal quantifier 'for all' to make a statement that applies to every number greater than 2.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'there exists' and 'for all'?
options: There exists' asserts the existence of at least one element, while 'for all' asserts the presence of every element.
There exists' asserts the presence of every element, while 'for all' asserts the existence of at least one element.
There exists' is used for sets and 'for all' is used for numbers.
There exists' is used for numbers and 'for all' is used for sets.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'There exists' asserts the existence of at least one element, while 'for all' asserts the presence of every element.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is an example of using 'there exists'?
options: All numbers greater than 2 are odd.
There is a cat in this room.
This book is on the table.
There are students in this class.
questions.explanation: The statement 'There is a cat in this room.' uses the existential quantifier 'there exists' to make a statement that asserts the existence of at least one cat in the room.
